title: 10 best herbal essential oils for gastroparesis
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for gastroparesis are a natural remedy that helps alleviate symptoms of this condition by promoting digestion, reducing nausea, and improving gut motility. The best herbal essential oils for gastroparesis include Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Cinnamomum verum, Foeniculum vulgare, Ginkgo biloba, Cuminum cyminum, Curcuma longa, Angelica archangelica, Syzygium aromaticum, and Pimpinella anisum. If left untreated, gastroparesis can lead to severe abdominal pain, weight loss, dehydration, and fatigue, significantly impacting daily life, limiting mobility, and affecting mental well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree essential oils helps with gastroparesis because of its unique composition, rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. The oil's properties help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and spasms that can exacerbate gastroparesis symptoms. Additionally, the oil's ability to stimulate digestion and improve gastric motility can help to alleviate nausea, vomiting, and abdominal discomfort associated with the condition. Overall, maidenhair tree essential oils may provide natural relief for individuals suffering from gastroparesis.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic property helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps associated with Gastroparesis, allowing for smoother digestion and reduced discomfort.

Carminative: The carminative property helps to ease gas, bloating, and flatulence, which are common symptoms of Gastroparesis, by stimulating the release of gases from the digestive tract and reducing pressure on the stomach and intestines.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory property may help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to Gastroparesis symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving symptoms of gastroparesis such as abdominal pain and discomfort.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation associated with gastroparesis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves from healthy trees and rinse them thoroughly under cool running water.
Blend one cup of chopped ginkgo biloba leaves with two cups of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to facilitate the infusion process.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Store the ginkgo biloba essential oil in a cool dark place for at least two weeks before using it topically or aromatically.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with gastroparesis because of its natural ability to soothe and relax the muscles in the stomach. The menthol content in peppermint oil can help to calm down the spasms and cramps associated with gastroparesis, allowing for easier digestion and reduced nausea. Additionally, peppermint oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce inflammation in the gut, which can exacerbate gastroparesis symptoms. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, peppermint essential oils may provide relief from gastroparesis-related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Peppermint oil's digestive stimulant property helps to improve digestion, relieve bloating, and reduce nausea, which can alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis.
Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of peppermint oil help to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing cramps, spasms, and pain associated with gastroparesis.

Anti-Inflammatory: Peppermint oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to gastroparesis symptoms such as abdominal pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of peppermint are used because they contain a high concentration of menthol, which helps to relax the muscles in the stomach and alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis.

Barks: The barks of peppermint are used due to their rich content of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with gastroparesis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or a trusted supplier in the early morning.
Rinse the harvested peppermint leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and impurities.
Steam distill 100g of peppermint leaves with 200ml of distilled water for 30 minutes to release the essential oil.
Separate the obtained essential oil from the hydrosol by decantation or filtering through a coffee filter.
Transfer the collected essential oil to dark glass bottles and store it in a cool place away from direct sunlight for 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with gastroparesis because its natural properties stimulate digestive enzymes, reducing inflammation and improving gut motility. The oil's bioactive compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, work to increase the secretion of digestive juices and relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, allowing food to pass more efficiently through the digestive system. This can help alleviate symptoms like nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain commonly associated with gastroparesis, promoting a sense of relief and comfort for those affected.

Antioxidant: Ginger's antioxidant properties can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is common in gastroparesis patients, and may also help protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage.

Anti-inflammatory: Herbal ginger essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis such as abdominal pain, bloating, and discomfort.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh rhizomes of zingiber officinale and wash them gently with clean water.
Dry the harvested rhizomes in a warm oven at 60°c for 24 hours or until they are brittle.
Grind the dried rhizomes into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part of the ground zingiber officinale powder with 2 parts of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for at least 14 days to allow the essential oils to infuse.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried l avandula angustifolia flowers in a ratio of 10 parts by weight to one part of carrier oil.
Steam distill the mixture using a clevenger apparatus at atmospheric pressure for 2-3 hours.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.
Store the finished lavandula angustifolia essential oil in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight and heat sources.
Use 5-7 drops of the essential oil blended with 1 teaspoon of carrier oil applied topically to the stomach area twice daily.

Dry the plant material in a warm, dry place away from direct sunlight.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh elettaria cardamomum pods in the morning after dew has evaporated to ensure highest oil content.

Filter the extracted essential oil through a coffee filter paper or cheesecloth into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for storage.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gastroparesis with herbal essential oils is by combining peppermint, ginger, and fennel oil. supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gastroparesis that are treated with herbal essential oils are Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS), Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD), and Dyspepsia. Essential oils such as peppermint, ginger, and fennel have been shown to alleviate symptoms like bloating, abdominal pain, and digestive discomfort associated with these conditions. These oils can also help reduce inflammation, soothe the digestive tract, and regulate bowel movements, providing relief from chronic gut issues.

Filter the extracted essential oil through a sterile filter paper to remove any remaining impurities.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils helps with gastroparesis because of its anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. The oil's active compounds, such as curcumin, have been shown to improve digestion by increasing gut motility and reducing inflammation in the stomach. This can help alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis, including nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Additionally, the oil's digestive-aiding properties may help stimulate digestion and absorption of nutrients, leading to improved overall digestive health and reduced symptoms of gastroparesis.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: They help with gastroparesis by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, improving gut motility, and alleviating symptoms of nausea and vomiting.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): It helps with gastroparesis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, reducing oxidative stress, and modulating the gut-brain axis to improve digestion.

Bis-demethoxycurcumin (BDMC): It helps with gastroparesis by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, reducing symptoms of gastroparesis, and improving gut health through its ability to modulate the gut microbiome.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part for making essential oils due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound that has been shown to alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis.
Roots: Roots of Curcuma longa are also used to create essential oils, as they contain bioactive compounds that may help in managing gastroparesis symptoms.
Leaves: Leaves of Curcuma longa are utilized to produce essential oils, which may possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate gastroparesis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh curcuma longa rhizomes from a pesticide-free field and dry them in a warm shaded area for 2 days.
Steam distill the dried rhizomes in a distillation apparatus at 100°c for 4 hours to obtain a mixture of essential oil and water.
Separate the essential oil from the water mixture by decantation and collect the essential oil in a clean glass container.
Filter the essential oil through a 10µm filter paper to remove any impurities and particulate matter.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at a temperature not exceeding 30°c.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica essential oils helps with gastroparesis because its anti-inflammatory and carminative properties help to soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms such as nausea and bloating. The oil's ability to stimulate digestion and improve gut motility also aids in regulating bowel movements and reducing symptoms of gastroparesis. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive system, promoting a healthy and balanced gut environment. This ultimately leads to improved overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Ligustilide: This sesquiterpene lactone helps with gastroparesis by relaxing the smooth muscles in the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and improving digestion.

Butylphthalide: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and smooth muscle-relaxing properties, which may help alleviate the symptoms of gastroparesis such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.

Angelol: This phenolic compound has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help protect the gastrointestinal tract from damage and inflammation associated with gastroparesis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part of Angelica archangelica for making essential oils, as they contain a high concentration of angelica root oil, which has been traditionally used to alleviate digestive issues.
Leaves: The leaves of Angelica archangelica contain angelica leaf oil, which has been used to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms associated with gastroparesis.
Stems: The stems of Angelica archangelica can also be used to produce angelica stem oil, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 fresh angelica archangelica roots in the early morning or late afternoon when temperatures are cooler.
Wash the roots thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and impurities then dry them with clean paper towels.
Steam distill the dried roots in a copper still using a 15% water ratio for 1-2 hours.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a separator or by refrigeration overnight.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Syzygium aromaticum essential oils helps with gastroparesis because it has been found to have carminative and anti-inflammatory properties. The oils' bioactive compounds, such as eugenol and beta-caryophyllene, help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can alleviate gastroparesis symptoms like nausea and abdominal pain. Additionally, the oils' ability to relax the digestive muscles and improve gut motility can help to regulate digestion and alleviate gastroparesis symptoms, promoting a more balanced and healthy digestive system.
remedy_constituents: Eugenol: A natural antioxidant that helps in reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of gastroparesis.
Caryophyllene: A sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory properties, which helps in improving gut motility and reducing nausea associated with gastroparesis.

Cinnamaldehyde: A phenolic aldehyde that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce inflammation and infection in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of gastroparesis.
remedy_parts: Buds: Used to make essential oils due to their high concentration of eugenol, which can help alleviate stomach cramps and spasms associated with gastroparesis.
Leaves: Utilized to make essential oils that contain compounds like eugenol and beta-caryophyllene, which may help stimulate digestive enzymes and alleviate nausea.
Fruits: Employed to produce essential oils rich in eugenol, which could potentially aid in relieving stomach discomfort and promoting digestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh syzygium aromaticum flowers at dawn when they are fully open and the fragrance is strongest.
Steam distill the freshly harvested flowers using a 1:3 ratio of flowers to water for 2 hours.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a 5-micron filter paper to remove any impurities and improve clarity.
Store the resulting syzygium aromaticum essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Pimpinella anisum essential oils helps with gastroparesis because it has been traditionally used to alleviate digestive issues, including bloating and indigestion, associated with gastroparesis. The oil's carminative properties help to ease digestion and relieve symptoms such as nausea and abdominal discomfort. Additionally, its anisic acid content has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, further alleviating gastroparesis symptoms. This makes Pimpinella anisum essential oil a potential natural remedy for individuals suffering from gastroparesis.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: This terpene helps with gastroparesis by stimulating digestive enzymes and improving gut motility.
Linalool: This monoterpene has a relaxing effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and spasms that contribute to gastroparesis.
Apigenin: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to protect the gut lining and improve digestion in individuals with gastroparesis.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Pimpinella anisum are commonly used to make essential oils due to their high anethole content, which has been shown to help alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis by relaxing the muscles of the digestive tract.

Leaves: The leaves of the plant are used to make essential oils that contain compounds like limonene and beta-pinene, which may help to improve digestion and alleviate nausea associated with gastroparesis.

Fruits: The fruits of Pimpinella anisum, specifically the seeds and oil contained within, are used to make essential oils that have been traditionally used to aid digestion and relieve symptoms of gastroparesis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh anisum flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated and the sun is not intense.
Steam distill the anisum flowers in a distillation apparatus for 3-4 hours with a 5:1 water to plant ratio.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distillation apparatus and transfer it to a clean glass container.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities or contaminants from the distillation process.
Store the herbal essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dry place.
